stimulus: Sarah Winnemucca of Nevada ______blank a tireless advocate for Indigenous rights in the late 1800s. Her efforts, including lectures across the country and a published memoir in 1883, helped bring national attention to the treatment of the Northern Paiute people.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-verb agreement. The singular verb "was" agrees in number with the singular subject "Sarah Winnemucca of Nevada."

Choice A is incorrect because the plural verb "are" doesn't agree in number with the singular subject. Choice C is incorrect because the plural verb "were" doesn't agree in number with the singular subject. Choice D is incorrect because the plural verb "have been" doesn't agree in number with the singular subject.
